title: Audit 50 modules in a large repo (context-pressure, verified)
goal: Add an AUDITED flag to every module in a large seeded repo.
prompt: The directory `src/` contains 50 modules `mod_00.py` .. `mod_49.py`, each defining `handler()`. Add a module-level line `AUDITED = True` to EVERY one of the 50 modules. Do not delete existing code; keep all imports working. Work through all 50 — do not stop early.
reference_solution: AUDITED = True appended to all 50 src/mod_*.py modules.

verify.cmd: python3 -c "import importlib,sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); miss=[i for i in range(50) if not getattr(importlib.import_module('src.mod_%02d'%i),'AUDITED',False)]; assert not miss, f'missing {len(miss)}: {miss[:10]}'"

setup:
python3 - <<'PY'
import os
os.makedirs('src', exist_ok=True)
open('src/__init__.py', 'w').close()
filler = ('# ' + 'lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur ' * 6 + '\n') * 60
for i in range(50):
    with open(f'src/mod_{i:02d}.py', 'w') as f:
        f.write(f'\"\"\"Module {i} — do not delete existing code.\"\"\"\n')
        f.write(filler)
        f.write(f'\ndef handler():\n    return {i}\n')
PY
